If your establishment keeps records of only the hours paid or if you have employees who are not paid by the hour, please estimate the hours that the employees actually worked. ### High Work Hours:

Are your hours and/or employee count entered correctly? The total number of hours reported are considerably *HIGHER* than the average annual hours typically worked by full-time employees.

Please validate that you have entered your correct employee count and/or total hours. If correct, provide a brief explanation for approval (this will be visible to your client connections). Otherwise, please correct your answers.

### Low Work Hours:

Are your hours and/or employee count entered correctly? The total number of hours reported are considerably *LOWER* than the average annual hours typically worked by full-time and part-time employees.

Please validate that you have entered your correct employee count and/or total hours. If correct, provide a brief explanation for approval (this will be visible to your client connections). Otherwise, please correct your answers.

Fatalities: Any work-related incident resulting in the death of an employee, either immediately or within one year due to complications directly associated with the incident.
Serious Injuries: Injuries resulting in permanent or long-term damage such as loss of limb, loss of vision, hearing, or other critical functions, including cases of clinical death where the person survives.
Lost Time Injuries: Incidents resulting in temporary incapacity for work with an absence from work for one or more days following the injury.
Medical Treatment Injuries: Work incidents that do not cause absence but require medical intervention beyond simple first aid.
Restricted Work Injuries: Incidents that result in the worker being temporarily assigned to lighter or modified duties due to injury, but without full absence from work.
First Aid cases: Minor injury incidents that require only basic first aid at the workplace without further medical treatment or lost time.
Near Misses/Dangerous Events: Events that under slightly different circumstances could have resulted in injury or damage, but did not. These are reportable for preventive measures.
Act No. 124 Collection of Laws on Occupational Safety and Health Protection; Section 8; National Labour Inspectorate Guidance
